rationale: Both evaluations land on the same 6/10 score and make substantively identical points: some C++ experience but not in capital markets/fixed income, strong general software background, and platform/domain gaps. The wording differs (e.g., 'Windows platform' framed slightly differently, SQL Server/Oracle called out explicitly in the variant), but these are reorderings and rephrasings of the same concerns, well within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing in the variant keys off the candidate's age, the gap duration, or any demographic signal — the shortened career-gap window produced no penalty or age-based reasoning.
